summary: This is an event announcement for the Alt Banking group, promoting a talk by British author and journalist Nafeez Ahmed on mass surveillance and civilizational crisis. Ahmed, an international security scholar who writes for The Guardian, will discuss the collapse of old paradigms and potential for new ones. The post provides background on Ahmed's work covering interconnected environmental, energy, and economic crises, and links to his investigative pieces on Pentagon surveillance programs and data-mining tools targeting activists.
key_points: Nafeez Ahmed will speak at Alt Banking about mass surveillance and civilizational crisis
Ahmed is an international security scholar who writes for The Guardian on interconnected global crises
Recommended reading includes his Pentagon pieces on civil breakdown preparation and activist tracking
The talk frames current surveillance as part of a broader systemic paradigm shift
money_quotes: Mass Surveillance and the Crisis of Civilization: The inevitable collapse of the old paradigm and the potential for the rise of the new
Pentagon preparing for mass civil breakdown
Exposed: Pentagon Funds New Data-Mining Tools To Track and Kill Activists
key_insight: This event announcement highlights growing concern about the intersection of mass surveillance, data-mining tools, and their potential use against civilian activists.
